posts.text:
BRAVA! SHE should be NY senator


Kadia Goba (@kadiagoba)

.@AOC to reporters just now: "There are members of Congress who have won Trump held districts in some of the most difficult territories in the United States; who walked the plank and took innumerable risks in order to defend the American people...just to see some Senate Democrats even consider acquiescing to Elon Musk. I think it is a huge slap in the face, and I think that there's a wide sense of betrayal."



— https://nitter.net/kadia

BIG NEWS. I have left the Post. Corporate and billionaire media are failing to meet the moment. Please subscribe and join the fight. http://contrarian.substack.comAnd because I want to be true to my values I am leaving X. I refuse to enable the Elon-Trump presidency. contrarian.substack.com/

Our democracy needs more defenders . as fror LA: LA often gets a bad rap. Yes, the traffic, the homeless problem and the high cost of living (especially the gap between rich and poor) can demoralize even lifetime residents; the fixation on superficial luxuries and appearances can exasperate you. But none of those negatives — nor the serial natural disasters — define LA. It has long been one of the most diverse (and hence creative) places in the country, its vibrant history and rich cultural trad

I said all I had to say -on the climate, the media, truth.


The Washington Post (@washingtonpost)

On @PostLive’s “First Look,” associate editor @CapehartJ speaks with The Post’s @Cat_Zakrzewski, @jimgeraghty and @JRubinBlogger about Elon Musk roiling EU politics, President Jimmy Carter’s legacy and fires in L.A. 

Watch more: wapo.st/4aadKB2 nitter.net/i/broadcasts/1MYxNMgdX…



— https://nitter.net/washingtonpost/status/1877717014728339910#m
NY advice, observation and self help tips! piped.video/watch?v=StFXI0…
A perfect Christmas message of hope and fellowship piped.video/cMd_xlvTKIw

A depressingly high number of elected Democrats are declaring their intent to find “common ground” with President-elect Donald Trump and his crackpot Cabinet picks. Their naive, tone-deaf declarations epitomize an infatuation with bipartisanship for bipartisanship’s sake. Sometimes, it’s better not to bend the knee before the bidding even gets underway.
washingtonpost.com/opinions/…

Democrats should decide on some red lines they will never cross. Whatever the merits of the underlying legislation, they can agree, for example, to reject life-threatening gambits (e.g., vaccine bans). Likewise, they must be emphatic that they will never approve blatantly unconstitutional measures (using the military to round up immigrants, a national abortion ban, retaliation against political enemies). Similarly, Democrats must be clear about the sort of measures, imperfections and all, they l
Jen Rubin: Dems Need To Learn There Is No Common Ground With Trump.. stop saying this nonsense!! piped.video/EeSnjtvilps?si=JitA… via @YouTube

If a news organization offers up financial tribute or shies from endorsing an opponent, readers and viewers have every right to question its impartiality, aggressiveness and spine.

The sort of behavior we have witnessed from many legacy outlets will not help win back audiences who have lost faith in them. (Progressives are horrified; right-wingers will never patronize them.) Maintaining financial and personal distance from the president, whom news organizations are obligated to investigate and
Isn’t birthright citizenship a right? I’m answering your questions. wapo.st/49J2HOR

Abortion advocates see a few wins — and more tragedies wapo.st/4iH1tba  Millions of women living under abortion bans are counting on Democrats to hold Republicans responsible for dangerous forced-birth laws and to underscore that state abortion protections are not sufficient to prevent the rise in maternal and infant deaths.
Democrats need to pound home the message: For women and infants, theirs is the only “pro-life” party.

Biden must ignore pardon myths and protect Patel’s and Trump’s targets wapo.st/41AWiDy If ordinary citizens face retribution for daring to testify against powerful bullies, few will do so. To preserve the justice system, to encourage people to provide evidence, Biden should grant them amnesty — in effect setting up a witness protection program. It is the least he can do for selfless Americans

McConnell unquestionably enabled the architect of “America First” to return to power (even endorsing him in 2024). Now he has the chutzpah to wring his hands about American isolationism and a possible Russian triumph in its war of aggression.... here is how he partially atones
wapo.st/3DcNs4O
AIPAC spent heavily in IL 9th. Their chosen candidate is running third. The guy they went after is winning. Total embarrassment and well deserved. They need to go self-reflect.
what a shocker... Bari Weiss is a failure: variety.com/2026/tv/news...
dead on... I would only add every Israeli pm has tried to get an American pres to do this and Trump was the only one dumb enough to say yes
is he saying this war is going to be almost a YEAR??

utter straw man to say opponents dont recognize Iran danger or have alternatives: the JCPOA plus extreme sanctions. That's the alternative. ridiculous defense of the worst possible option (war) www.theatlantic.com/ideas/2026/0...
the right conclusion is not that it is Israel's fault. It is Trump's

This mimics Jim Crow legislation (e.g., passposrts can cost $165, is akin to a poll tax). It's blatantly unconstitutional, a gambit by the most lawless and corrupt regime in history (whose identity is rooted in election denial) to take over elections from the states open.substack.com/pub/contrari...

The “solution” in search of a problem is thinly disguised plot to sow massive confusion and disenfranchise as many voters as needed to keep them in power. That’s their election strategy to counteract the public’s overwhelming disapproval of their MAGA agenda  open.substack.com/pub/contrari...
well, well  youtu.be/OMJrMxO3Gx4?...
of course they are cause Trump has no effing idea what he is doing  wapo.st/4lHm015
our very own @timdickinson.bsky.social on the frightening role of Christian nationalism in our war(s)... this is why religion and politics need an arms length relationship: slate.com/podcasts/wha...

Going forward, policymakers should remember Trump’s blunders and do the opposite. Finally, voters must remember that regardless of what they say, Republicans cannot resist a Middle East war and invariably underestimate its cost in blood and treasure.
open.substack.com/pub/contrari...

after the fighting, the region will be more volatile, the human cost breathtaking, and the need for an agreement to contain Iran’s nuclear program that much more essential. To both allies and foes, the U.S. will appear unreliable and disingenuous
open.substack.com/pub/contrari...
The realization that regime change is a pipe dream, which U.S. presidents have learned repeatedly, has swamped Trump’s megalomania and Netanyahu’s dream of “forever” removing an Iranian threat.

This catastrophic blunder stems from Trump’s delusion that a forty-seven-year-old Islamic regime insulated by layers of bureaucracy, an enormous military, an entrenched ideology, and a fervent national identity could be bombed out of existence.
open.substack.com/pub/contrari...
Best host. Every time.
Join us on Wednesday!!
Yanar Mohammed, 65, Iraqi Women’s Rights Advocate, Is Killed by Gunmen www.nytimes.com/2026/03/12/w... breathtaking
He was wrong www.wsj.com/politics/nat...
